action.hypothesis: A gated SAE that decouples latent support selection from reconstruction magnitude can beat the current JumpReLU anchor at K=128 by learning a separate sigmoid routing branch while staying initialization-close to plain ReLU top-k.
next_hypotheses: If gated shows better proxy FVU at acceptable throughput, stabilize the family by exposing and tuning gate temperature/init strength in a follow-up.
If gated quality is promising but throughput regresses, keep the family alive and do a performance-focused follow-up rather than discarding the architecture.
If gated fails to improve proxy FVU, move on to a different routing-focused family instead of more JumpReLU-local sweeps.
notes_to_memory: Added a new `gated` SAE family under `sparsify/` with a second encoder branch that produces sigmoid gates for support selection.
The gate branch is initialized with zero weights and a positive bias so the prototype starts close to plain ReLU top-k rather than an aggressively sparse regime.
Current execution-layer hooks do not expose gated-specific env knobs, so this first prototype relies on default `gated_temperature=1.0` and `gated_init_logit=2.0`.
action.self_review: This is a clean single-family prototype and respects the proxy-first policy, but it adds a second encoder matmul so throughput may regress; that risk is acceptable for one exploratory proxy round because recent local JumpReLU tuning already failed.
action.summary: Prototype a new `gated` architecture at the K=128 anchor by adding a separate sigmoid routing branch, keeping all other launch settings at baseline so the round cleanly measures whether decoupled support selection can open a better quality regime than JumpReLU.
